programmes.show_description: When mother Mary Davis is found murdered, the case spans decades. Bleeding edge forensics could hold the answers. S5, Ep17
The Judge shares a phrase that her husband has not been able to utter. S24, Ep5
After a heated argument with his 34-year-old son, a man allegedly backs out of an agreement to help his ex. S24, Ep6
When asked to produce receipts for property damage, a man asks for the Judge's patience due to his loss of a relative in a mass shooting. S24, Ep7
When motorists offer up wildly different collision stories, the timing of their damage claims makes all the difference. S22, Ep120
Friends for over 40 years end their relationship after a deal to renovate a home escalates into disorderly conduct and alleged assault. S22, Ep121
Unwed parents living with family members are accused of skipping out on rent; they say they were harassed daily and had to get the police involved. S22, Ep122
Two Chihuahuas are found dead after a neighbour's American Bullies allegedly bust through a doggie door and attack them. S22, Ep123
A woman accuses her landlord of inappropriate behaviour; she denies owing him for rent and breaking her lease. S22, Ep124
The Judge discovers an intriguing story of alleged fraud and vandalism when an unsuspecting motorist calls for help. S22, Ep125
After a couple is asked to pay up for an unauthorised tenant, they complain about leaks, mould and sewage issues. S22, Ep126
A bar school owner claims his former employee and his fiancé stole content from his website and posted it as their own. S16, Ep51
When a gang of teenagers approaches her Texan home in search of her only son, Debbie Fleming leaps to his defence and shoots a 16-year-old dead. S3, Ep4
Series presented by Nick Knowles which shows the split-second moments when everyday events are transformed into disasters and meets the people who lived to tell the tale. S2, Ep12
Series presented by Nick Knowles which shows the split-second moments when everyday events are transformed into disasters and meets the people who lived to tell the tale. S2, Ep13
Innovative, value-for-money products brought directly to you at home. Shop 'til you drop whilst avoiding the hustle and bustle of the High Street.
A man claims his friend constructively evicted him when she turned off the electricity; she says he acted entitled and disrespectful. S22, Ep92
Divorcees fight over the return of tools and the cost of their 17-year-old twins' sports activities. S22, Ep93
A woman hires a friend to sell an $8,000 Harley Davidson for her, but she says she never saw a penny once the deal was done. S22, Ep94
An independent hair stylist accuses her landlord of treating her like an employee rather than a renter. S22, Ep95
When a co-worker 'disrespects' his angry, broom-wielding fiancée, he beats him up; the Judge makes him pay for his foolish choice. S24, Ep197
All hell breaks loose at a custody exchange when a young man realises his alleged baby mama is cheating on him; he orders a paternity test. S24, Ep198
Jill Zimmerman sues former Geometry tutor David Kaspar, for the return of fees she paid him to tutor her daughter. S24, Ep199
A beer can hits a woman in the head, and she suffers a concussion; when she reunites with the boyfriend who supposedly threw the can, she is sued by an admirer for alleged loans. S16, Ep1
A woman sues a cement mason for mistakenly tearing up her driveway; the mason claims he was a victim of a 'jack-hammering scam'. S24, Ep8
Before coming to court, a man receives a ruling that bars him entry to his own home; he accuses his ex-lover of lying and stealing. S24, Ep9
A daughter is outraged that her mother called authorities to perform a welfare check on her four children; the Judge sets her straight. S24, Ep10
Smart thieves figure out how to open a locked cabinet and get away with a man's prized gun valued at $4,500. S22, Ep127
A father calls his daughter a thief after she allegedly reneges on a promise to pay him for a customized car. S22, Ep128
A landlord claims a woman unexpectedly moved in with her son and set up a 'gestapo desk' from which she spied on his home's residents. S22, Ep129
When a retired pest control worker sprays insect killer on a shared tree, his neighbours claim they suffered serious respiratory injury. S22, Ep130
A writer claims she submitted articles that her editor never paid her for; he says the writing was not factual, and that he wasn't paid by his clients. S22, Ep131
A woman demands thousands in payback for a valuable truck; her friend says she traded an antique, hand carved oak table for the vehicle. S22, Ep132
A daycare provider says her attempt to improve a playground resulted in substandard concrete work; she sues for damages. S22, Ep133
A senior citizen is accused of assaulting another senior at a Ladies' Auxiliary VFW meeting and is escorted out of her hotel room by police. She sues for damages. S16, Ep14
After meeting a mechanic at a bowling alley, a woman ends up suing him for allegedly selling her broken-down BMW without her permission; he claims he was forced to get rid of it. S16, Ep15
A mother's allegedly inappropriate behaviour with her daughter's fiancé, drinking problems and damage caused by Chihuahuas come into play in a fight over fishing poles. S16, Ep16
After ruining her credit, a woman demands that her boyfriend's adult son return the car she co-signed for him or give her money to pay it off. S16, Ep17
In the quiet town of Hyde, Greater Manchester, patients of respected GP Harold Shipman begin dying in unusually high numbers. S2, Ep6
An NHS manager cooks up a scam with his mates to line their pockets with two hundred and fifty thousand pounds. S1, Ep4
A fresh coat of snow helps investigators uncover secrets and lies surrounding the brutal murder of aspiring nurse, Nancy Billiter. S3, Ep18
Thomas Downey is murdered in Bath. DNA and CCTV lead to Darrell Richards being jailed in 2015. S1, Ep3
Serial rapes spur fear in a city in North Carolina. Years later new forensic science finds the perpetrator. S5, Ep18

A lesbian couple is stood up by their DJ the night before their wedding; the DJ claims they texted him 100 times a day to get their money back. S16, Ep52
43-year-old mother of two Susan Bailey is found dead in her own home with stab wounds. Her daughter Jennifer and her boyfriend Paul Henson are found guilty of murder S3, Ep5

A man accused of coming into court with unclean hands regarding a botched co-op deal is put in his place after talking back to Her Honor. S22, Ep109
A young man sets off to Paris with his girlfriend only to allegedly beg mom for money once he gets there; she sues him for payback. S22, Ep110
When a young man exhibits allegedly poor restaurant manners, an outraged father takes justice into his own hands. S22, Ep111
Bricks are thrown and assault charges are filed when two women fight over the same unemployed man; the Judge sets them straight. S22, Ep112
A woman sues her former hairdresser for an unpaid loan to hire a divorce attorney; she claims she was paying off the loan by styling her hair for free. S16, Ep2
An alleged threat to report a dirt bike as stolen leads to a woman returning the bike to its owner despite being on time in her payments; she sues him for the return of payments she made. S16, Ep3
A woman is suing her sister for damages to an SUV that was in her name; her sister says she repossessed the vehicle two years ago. S16, Ep4
While serving in Iraq, a man meets a woman on Facebook whom he begins dating when he returns home. Both single parents, they end up fighting in court over money. S16, Ep5
A man comes to the rescue of his disabled mother while her daughter allegedly reneges on payback of a loan for her and her four children. S24, Ep11
While crossing the street in a motorised wheelchair, a woman is struck by an uninsured vehicle and suffers severe road rash. S24, Ep12
The Judge openly admits she dislikes a married man being sued by his ex-girlfriend; she says he went back to his wife and took her truck with him. S24, Ep13
An attorney claims he succeeded in having a difficult tenant evicted for his landlord client; he sues for the alleged remainder of his fees. S22, Ep134
A man claims a large Australian Shepherd pushed through a fence gap to bite his dog's paw; his neighbours say it was impossible. S22, Ep135
The Judge is incredulous when a man pre-emptively educates himself about how to prevent his Pit Bull from maiming him. S22, Ep136
All hell breaks loose when a woman's handyman starts dating one of her tenants; she files a restraining order and sues him for alleged vandalism. S22, Ep137
After a young man is rescued by a family friend, he makes a deal to move in and pay her back; she says he was like a son but sues him anyway. S22, Ep138
An evening of tequila drinking evolves into a reckless car ride that ends with an alleged collision. S22, Ep139
Squirrels in an attic, trash in the basement and tenant identity confusion come into play when a woman sues her landlord. S22, Ep140
A woman who claims her ex-fiancé wasn't ready to get married sues him for alleged loans to buy a motorcycle, make a mortgage payment, and pay his tuition. S16, Ep18
A man is accused of making homophobic comments and being rude to a fellow roommate; he sues his ex-roommate for the return of rent and a security deposit. S16, Ep19
A $5,000 marijuana drug deal gone wrong is disputed by ex-roommates who fight over alleged damages as a result of a police raid. S16, Ep20
After eight weeks of work, a young man says he was injured on the job and sues his boss for $5,000 for an alleged wrongful termination and a workman's comp claim. S16, Ep21
Vera Hudson was a well-known, well-loved member of her Hull community. So when she had stopped answering her door, neighbours grew worried. S2, Ep7
An accounts worker at a cancer hospital syphons off six hundred and forty-two thousand pounds of NHS money. S1, Ep5
Heavy summer rain, intensified by the 1998 El Niño, washes a mutilated body of an unknown woman to the surface of a creek in Spring Valley, New York. S3, Ep19
Elderly couple Benjamin and Dorothy were killed in their home in one of the most terrifying home invasions ever seen. S7, Ep3
